rows.notes: Clear page title at top left. No amounts in the row; handwriting is legible and unambiguous.
Clear section heading immediately under the title. No monetary columns present on this line.
Three narrow ruling-columns on right are well-aligned with this row: pounds '03', shillings '16', pence '8' are crisp and unambiguous.
Numbers '01 16 8' align perfectly with the three amount columns. Ink and digits clear.
Shillings '14' and pence '6' are clear; pounds read as '00' though leading zeroes are faint. Alignment with other rows consistent.
Pence '4' is visible in pence column. Pounds and shillings read '00' but the left-most pound-digit area is faint; chosen '00' because the small marks align with zeros in neighboring rows.
Pounds '04', shillings '12', pence '4' are clearly written and align with vertical ruling reference from totals row below.

Shillings column clearly shows '8'. The pence digit is faint and could be '2' or '6' (ink loop); chosen '2' based on alignment with the pence column and the shape of the small digit which leans toward '2'. Pound column reads as '00' but faint.
Shillings '12' and pence '6' align with the ruling and are readable; the leading pounds are '00' but the zeros are faint.
Shillings in this row are small ('1') and the pence '8' is legible; the pounds column is blank/zero. Slight irregular ink makes the small '1' less bold but placement leaves little ambiguity.
Clear '01' in pounds and '19' in shillings; pence '00' visible. Alignment with other totals confirms placement.
Numbers '01 14 8' are clear and neatly aligned in the three amount columns.

rows.description: To the Administrat of Grouc Cook the last year
rows.notes: Pounds '01' and shillings '10' are clear; pence '8' visible though the tail is slightly faint. Text is somewhat abbreviated but readable.

rows.description: To the present Cook for this year
rows.notes: Large, clear '2' in the pounds column and '00' in the shillings and pence columns. Very legible compared to surrounding figures.
Shillings '13' and pence '4' align with ruling; pounds read as '00'. The word 'pro Anno' and amount pairing are clear.
Amount '00 13 4' repeats in same alignment as previous rows and is legible.
Third consecutive entry with the same '00 13 4' amount; digits are clear and consistently placed.
Same amount pattern as prior lines ('00 13 4'); the text 'non graduat.' is faint but legible and the amounts align.
Pounds '01' and shillings '03' are visible; pence '00' reads as blank/zero. The word looks like 'Belde' (interpreted as 'Bede') but amount alignment is clear.
Numbers '01 6 8' are clear in their columns. Slight ink variation but digits unambiguous.
Shillings read as '15' and pence '00'; the Latin phrase is slightly contracted but identifiable. Alignment consistent with ruling lines.
This row appears to be an item (entry) but has no amounts written. The text is moderately faded; interpreted as an entry because it sits in the list under the headings.
Pounds '01' clearly written; shillings and pence '00'. The 'Consecration sermon' text is legible and the amounts align with ruling.
Large clear '20' in pounds column (two-digit pounds are distinct here). Shillings and pence read '00'. Text includes an annotation '10L' which matches the large pound figure.
Shillings '02' and pence '00' visible but small; pounds column blank/zero. Word reads as 'Corne prisey' (interpreted as corn price).
Line 'tenths of merton' present but no amount written. Ink for this description is lighter and the amount columns are blank for this row.

rows.description: synodally Cudl. Southn. Merton adv. Fest. pasch:
rows.notes: Shillings '07' is visible; pounds '00' and pence '00'. Text is abbreviated Latin; chosen amounts align with the narrow ruling columns.

This is a braced/grouped line: the written brace and 'Bocardo 5s 5s' suggest two 5s items summed; amount read as '00 10 00' in the aligned columns. Text is abbreviated; moderate ambiguity in exact wording but numeric sum '10s' aligns with columns.

A second braced/sub-line associated with 'Pris'ns' showing another '00 10 00'. The descriptive text is poorly formed/abbreviated (read as 'Custh?' or similar) so confidence on text is lower; the amount alignment is consistent with the previous braced entry.
Clear '01' in pounds and '10' in shillings columns. The pence column shows '00'. The word 'Pauperibus' is legible and aligns with the amount.
Shillings '02' and pence '00' are clear in the narrow columns; description 'Bibliorum clerico' readable though slightly abbreviated.
Final total at bottom right reads clearly as '48 12 10' underlined by a horizontal rule. Digits are well-formed and align with the column lines used to place other amounts.
